prompt:
You are creating a strict inspection checklist for a Quality Control system.
Based on the summary of a normal breakfast_box, generate 6 essential Yes/No questions to detect logical anomalies.

[ Description of breakfast_box ]
1. Components: Two tangerines, one nectarine (peach or apple), and a portion of granola/cereal mix with banana chips and almonds.
2. Quantities: Exactly two tangerines; exactly one peach or apple depending on the description variant used in each sample but always present as either fruit type within given constraints outlined above regarding specific placement rules applicable only under certain conditions specified earlier which dictate how many pieces need occupying space inside container along with what kind they represent respectively based upon coloration patterns etcetera!
3. Spatial Arrangement:
   - The breakfast box should be white plastic
   - Cereals occupy about half to three-quarters of the upper section at top-right corner designated by white boundaries;
   - Below cereals is an equal mix of sliced bananas & whole almonds filling up most lower area leaving some empty spaces around it.

4. Visual Appearance:

    * Tangerines have bright orange hue indicating ripeness suitable for consumption during morning hours after waking up early due natural sugars helping provide energy boost needed throughout day ahead!

    * Nectarine has reddish-yellow skin showing signs of ripeness without being overly ripe yet still sweet enough raw! Green hues near stem indicate freshness level too

    * Granola/Cereal Mixture contains light brown tones suggesting whole grain composition rich in fiber content essential part dietary regimen aiming towards maintaining overall health status over time period! May also contain nuts/seeds offering additional crunchiness texture contrast against softer fruit components found elsewhere within same package contents listed previously mentioned before now...

    * Banana Chips exhibit golden yellow shades denoting dehydrated state resulting from cooking process applied prior packaging step ensuring long shelf life while retaining nutritional value intact despite being dried out slightly compared fresh counterparts available at grocery stores nowadays commonly seen everywhere around us daily lives...

[ Normal Constraints for breakfast_box ]
- The breakfast box always contains exactly two tangerines and one nectarine that are always located on the left-hand side of the box.
- The ratio and relative position of the cereals and the mix of banana chips and almonds on the right-hand side are fixed.

Using ONLY the constraints listed in [ Normal Constraints for breakfast_box ] and [ Description of breakfast_box ], create exactly 6 simple and important YES/NO questions to determine whether the breakfast_box in the image is normal or abnormal. Ensure the questions are only based on visible characteristics, excluding any aspects that cannot be determined from the image. Also, simplify any difficult terms into easy-to-understand questions.
STRICT RULES:
- Must be answered with a simple "Yes" or "No".
- A "Yes" answer MUST mean the image is NORMAL. A "No" answer MUST mean it is an ANOMALY.
- Each question must evaluate exactly ONE logical constraint (e.g., check quantity OR check position, not both).
- Focus strictly on visible, objective characteristics (quantities, colors, specific positions, presence/absence).
- DO NOT use subjective words (e.g., "good", "proper", "normal").
- DO NOT use negative phrasing (e.g., use "Is the box full?" instead of "Is the box not empty?").

Output ONLY the questions, numbered 1 to 6. Do not add any introductory or concluding text.
(Q1) :
(Q2) :
(Q3) :
(Q4) :
(Q5) :
(Q6) :
response:
(Q1) Does the breakfast box contain exactly two tangerines and one nectarine?
(Q2) Are all three fruits located on the left-hand side of the box?
(Q3) Is there a portion of granola/cereal mix with banana chips and almonds occupying about half to three-quarters of the upper section at top-right corner designated by white boundaries in the image? 
(Q4) Do the cereals occupy most lower area leaving some empty spaces around it below them, as specified for spatial arrangement?
(Q5) Are bananas sliced into thin pieces while whole almonds are present within the mixture mentioned above?
(Q6) Does the cereal/mix have light brown tones suggesting whole grain composition rich in fiber content essential part dietary regimen aiming towards maintaining overall health status over time period! May also contain nuts/seeds offering additional crunchiness texture contrast against softer fruit components found elsewhere within same package contents listed previously mentioned before now...
parsed_questions: Does the breakfast box contain exactly two tangerines and one nectarine?
Are all three fruits located on the left-hand side of the box?
Is there a portion of granola/cereal mix with banana chips and almonds occupying about half to three-quarters of the upper section at top-right corner designated by white boundaries in the image?
Do the cereals occupy most lower area leaving some empty spaces around it below them, as specified for spatial arrangement?
Are bananas sliced into thin pieces while whole almonds are present within the mixture mentioned above?
